evals.prompt: Can you evaluate this empirical machine-learning-for-drug-discovery paper across all the research-quality dimensions and give me numeric scores plus an overall assessment?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scholar-eval applying the ScholarEval framework: identifies work type (empirical paper) and comprehensive scope, evaluates each core dimension (problem formulation, literature review, methodology, data/sources, analysis, results, writing, citations) with strengths/weaknesses, assigns 5-point dimension scores, optionally uses scripts/calculate_scores.py for aggregate scoring, and synthesizes an overall assessment with prioritized actionable recommendations.

files.name: ml_drug_discovery_paper.txt
files.content: Title: Deep Graph Networks for Binding Affinity Prediction. Abstract: We propose a GNN architecture trained on a kinase inhibitor dataset and report improved RMSE over baselines. Methods, results, and discussion sections follow. (truncated fixture)

evals.prompt: I only want a focused evaluation of the methodology and research design of my survey study — rigor, validity, reproducibility, and a rubric score for that dimension. Don't grade the writing.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scholar-eval in a targeted scope on a single dimension: assesses Methodology & Research Design (appropriateness for research questions, rigor and validity, reproducibility/transparency, ethics, limitations acknowledgment) against the ScholarEval rubric, gives a 5-point score with strengths and areas for improvement, and does not score unrelated dimensions like writing.

assertions.value: Scopes the evaluation to the methodology dimension with a rubric score and does not grade writing/presentation.

evals.prompt: Score my literature review chapter against publication standards and tell me whether it's ready to submit to a target journal, with quantitative ratings I can track across revisions.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-scholar-eval to benchmark publication readiness: evaluates the literature-review dimension (comprehensiveness, critical synthesis vs summarization, gap identification, currency) plus relevant dimensions with 5-point scores, assesses suitability for the target venue, and frames the quantitative scores so improvement can be tracked across revisions (e.g. producing a SCHOLAR_EVALUATION-style report).

assertions.value: Produces quantitative dimension scores and a publication-readiness judgment that can be re-run to track improvement across revisions.

evals.prompt: Write me a full peer-review report for this manuscript as if for the journal — summary, major and minor comments organized by section, and an accept/minor-revision/major-revision/reject recommendation letter to the editor.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-peer-review. The user wants a checklist-style narrative peer-review report with an editorial recommendation and a letter to the editor, not the rubric-based quantitative dimension scoring that ScholarEval produces.

evals.prompt: I'm not grading the whole paper — I just want to stress-test whether the central causal claim is actually supported by the evidence presented, and spot any logical fallacies or unwarranted leaps in the argument.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-scientific-thinking. The user wants evidence/claim-quality and argument-soundness analysis (fallacies, unwarranted inferences), not a structured multi-dimension rubric score of the scholarly work.
